name: Do One Thing
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 3: Functions
problem: Functions that perform multiple operations at different levels of abstraction are hard to name accurately, test in isolation, and reason about, and they grow unbounded as requirements change.
steps: Describe the function as a single TO paragraph: if the description requires and or or, the function does more than one thing.
Verify all statements in the function are at the same level of abstraction below the function name.
Extract any step that can be given a name that is not merely a restatement of its implementation.
Ensure the function cannot be meaningfully divided into sections.
Rename the function so it describes exactly the one thing it does.
